mnemonics: TSO: Timestamp Order (The Oldest Survives Order) - A younger transaction must wait for an older one to read or write a value, ensuring serializability based on age.
keyPoints: Each transaction is assigned a unique timestamp that determines its serialization order.
For a read operation: If a transaction tries to read a data item written by a younger transaction, it is rolled back. It must read the value from the most recent older writer.

For a write operation: If a transaction tries to write a data item that has been read by a younger transaction, it is rolled back to prevent a write-read conflict (Thomas' Write Rule can modify this behavior).
commonMistakes: Confusing the rollback condition for reads and writes (e.g., thinking a transaction is rolled back for reading a value from an older transaction, when the conflict is actually with a younger one).
Forgetting that a rolled-back transaction must be restarted with a new, larger timestamp, or the algorithm can lead to starvation.
